sentence_reviews.text: Interpretability methods attribute predictions or internal representations to input features, examples, concepts, directions, or learned components.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML and AIMA discuss model explanation/interpretability, features, representations, and attribution-style reasoning.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is a defensible synthesis; no specific interpretability survey is cited in this packet.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Feature attribution & interpretability is to connect Statistics & experimental design, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/AIMA support statistical evaluation, learned representations, and explainability concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible, but the downstream mechanistic-interpretability link is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports representation and interpretability foundations but does not directly establish mechanistic circuit analysis as downstream.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible taxonomy link; needs a mechanistic-interpretability source for stronger support.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/AIMA support statistical evaluation and model analysis methods.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Statistics & experimental design, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/AIMA support the dependency concepts but do not directly support the generic coverage/adversary limitation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Boilerplate limitation is not tailored to interpretability and should be narrowed or sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Feature attribution & interpretability measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Model explanation and attribution methods depend on measurement, estimation, and experimental comparisons covered by PML/AIMA.

sentence_reviews.text: Feature attribution & interpretability measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports learned representations and representation analysis as ML foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core dependency is plausible for representation-level interpretability.

sentence_reviews.text: Feature attribution & interpretability measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Causal inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA/PML support causal reasoning foundations; causal analysis can inform interpretability but is not always required.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship type is plausible, but common strength is better supported than a general dependency claim.

edge_reviews.notes: Statistics is a core foundation for attribution evaluation and interpretability studies.
Distributed representations are core for internal-representation interpretability.
Causal inference can support interpretability, but the cited sources do not directly establish this edge.
